Title: Transactivation of Sus1 and Sus2 by Opaque2 is an essential supplement to sucrose synthase-mediated endosperm filling in maize

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Reversibly cleaves sucrose with UDP to generate fructose and UDP-glucose; assayed in the sucrose-synthesis direction using fructose and UDP-glucose.
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Sucrose cleavage supplying carbon for starch and storage protein synthesis during endosperm filling
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Sucrose synthase-mediated source entry into endosperm storage metabolism
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: Upstream entry step supplying precursor and energy-efficient carbon flux
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Supplies precursor pool for starch synthesis and indirectly supports amino acid/protein synthesis
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Seed storage reserve determining kernel filling, size, weight and texture
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highly expressed in endosperm; major SUS gene active after 10 DAP
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. oen1 was cloned as Sh1, encoding a sucrose synthase required for normal endosperm filling. 2. sh1/oen1 strongly reduced SUS activity and starch accumulation and caused a shrunken kernel with enlarged seed cavity. 3. Combining sh1 with o2 or with sus1/sus2 further aggravated endosperm filling defects, showing Sh1 is the predominant SUS source in maize endosperm.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key control point for sucrose synthase-mediated endosperm filling; predominant SUS contributor in maize endosperm
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Loss of Sh1 greatly reduces SUS activity, lowers starch accumulation, increases soluble sugars, and causes shrunken kernels with reduced endosperm filling; it also enhances the o2 phenotype.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: EMS mutant mapping-by-sequencing; RT-PCR splice-defect validation; immunoblotting; allelism/complementation with sh1-ref; enzyme activity assay; metabolite and seed phenotype analyses
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Native promoters in maize; no transgenic promoter used for maize phenotyping
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Whole-genome resequencing for mapping-by-sequencing (no accession reported); prior RNA-seq cited from previous study, no accession reported here
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Normal endosperm development; developmental stage dependence (8–24 DAP, especially after 10 DAP)
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Reduced kernel weight, severe germination reduction, shorter plant height, hollow/shrunken endosperm
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Essential gene to preserve endosperm filling in breeding; avoid loss-of-function alleles when deploying o2/QPM backgrounds; SUS dosage should be maintained or stacked with regulatory support to sustain starch/protein deposition.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: oen1-1 and oen1-2 are splice-site alleles of Sh1. Sh1 contributes most SUS activity in endosperm; starch differences in multiple mutants were measured per flour mass rather than per seed, which may mask severity.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Reversibly cleaves sucrose to fructose and UDP-glucose.
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Sucrose cleavage supplying endosperm storage metabolism
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Supplies precursor pool and supplements Sh1-mediated carbon entry for starch/protein accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Seed storage reserve for kernel filling
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Expressed in endosperm at lower level than Sh1; transcript strongly reduced in o2 endosperm
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Sus1 is a maize SUS gene expressed in endosperm but dispensable alone for visible kernel phenotype. 2. O2 directly binds and strongly transactivates the Sus1 promoter, and Sus1 transcript drops to near-undetectable levels in o2. 3. Loss of Sus1 markedly enhances sh1 cavity and filling defects, demonstrating a supplementary role in endosperm filling.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Not established as rate-limiting alone; supplementary contributor to total SUS dosage during endosperm filling
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Single sus1 mutants show no obvious phenotype, but sus1 strongly enhances sh1-associated hollow endosperm and reduced filling.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RT-qPCR; dual-luciferase transactivation assay; EMSA; mutant combination analysis; SUS activity assay; starch measurement
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Sus1 native promoter tested in dual-luciferase reporter; 35S promoter used for O2 effector in Arabidopsis protoplasts
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Prior RNA-seq cited from previous study, no accession reported here
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Normal seed development; 12-DAP endosperm and post-10-DAP filling stage
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: When combined with sh1, severe kernel shrinkage and cavity enlargement
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful supportive target to maintain or boost SUS dosage in endosperm, especially in o2/QPM or compromised Sh1 backgrounds; could be stacked with O2-centered engineering.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: sus1 harbors a stop codon allele from MEMD in this study; stronger O2 responsiveness than Sh1 promoter (~20-fold).

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Reversibly cleaves sucrose to fructose and UDP-glucose.
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Sucrose cleavage supplying endosperm storage metabolism
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Supplies precursor pool and supplements major Sh1-mediated flux for storage accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Seed storage reserve for kernel filling
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Expressed in endosperm at lowest level among three Sus genes; transcript strongly reduced in o2
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Sus2 is a minor maize SUS gene with no visible single-mutant phenotype in endosperm. 2. O2 directly binds and very strongly transactivates the Sus2 promoter, and Sus2 transcript is nearly abolished in o2. 3. Loss of Sus2 enhances sh1 cavity defects, showing Sus2 provides supplementary SUS activity for endosperm filling.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Not established as rate-limiting alone; supplementary contributor to SUS dosage
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Single sus2 mutants are phenotypically normal, but loss of Sus2 aggravates sh1-associated endosperm cavity and filling defects.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RT-qPCR; dual-luciferase transactivation assay; EMSA; mutant combination analysis; SUS activity assay; starch measurement
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Sus2 native promoter tested in dual-luciferase reporter; 35S promoter used for O2 effector in Arabidopsis protoplasts
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Prior RNA-seq cited from previous study, no accession reported here
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Normal seed development; 12-DAP endosperm and post-10-DAP filling stage
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: When combined with sh1, causes more severe endosperm shrinkage and cavity enlargement
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate supportive gene for enhancing endosperm sucrose utilization, particularly where Sh1 function or O2 network is limiting; suitable for stacking strategies.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: sus2 harbors a stop codon allele from MEMD in this study; strongest O2 transactivation among the three Sus promoters (~100-fold) and contains two functional O2 boxes.

Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Sucrose cleavage entry into endosperm storage metabolism, coordinated with starch and storage protein synthesis
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Direct transcriptional activation via promoter binding to O2-box/ACGT cis-elements
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates all three Sus promoters, with much stronger activation of Sus1 and Sus2 than Sh1; loss of O2 markedly reduces Sus1 and Sus2 transcripts and mildly reduces Sh1 transcript
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Endosperm-specific developmental program; active after about 10 DAP
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Storage reserves that determine kernel filling, texture, weight and nutritional quality
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. O2 is an endosperm-specific bZIP TF that directly binds O2-box motifs in Sh1, Sus1 and Sus2 promoters. 2. O2 strongly activates Sus1 and Sus2 and more weakly activates Sh1, thereby sustaining total SUS activity during endosperm filling. 3. Loss of O2 reduces SUS activity and, together with sh1 loss, causes synergistically severe defects in starch accumulation, zein accumulation and kernel filling.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes endosperm filling by maintaining expression of sucrose synthase genes; o2 reduces protein content and modestly reduces SUS activity, and greatly worsens sh1-associated cavity and opacity phenotypes.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Genetic interaction analysis with sh1/oen1; RT-qPCR; dual-luciferase transactivation assay; EMSA; SUS activity assay; biochemical phenotyping
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Central regulator and essential supplementer of SUS-mediated endosperm filling; coordinates protein and starch synthesis through direct activation of sucrose-cleavage genes
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: 35S promoter used to express O2 in Arabidopsis protoplast transactivation assay
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: Previous RNA-seq and ChIP-seq datasets cited from earlier studies; no accession reported in this paper
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Normal endosperm development, particularly post-10-DAP filling stage
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: o2 reduces zein accumulation and causes opaque/non-vitreous endosperm; when combined with sh1, causes severe hollow kernels and lower kernel weight
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Key regulator for maize nutritional quality engineering; when using o2/QPM backgrounds, maintaining or enhancing O2-dependent SUS support may help preserve kernel filling and reduce negative texture/yield penalties; candidate for coordinated stacking with starch/protein pathway genes.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper emphasizes O2 as a central transcriptional regulator linking sucrose cleavage to storage protein and starch synthesis during endosperm filling. Strongest evidence concerns direct activation of Sus1 and Sus2.
